title: The Trinity
description: The doctrine that God exists eternally as three co-equal persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — united in one divine essence.

verses.text: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
verses.note: Baptismal formula names all three persons in one name

verses.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the communion of the Holy Ghost, be with you all. Amen. «The second epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi, a city of Macedonia, by Titus and Lucas.»
verses.note: Apostolic benediction invoking all three persons

verses.text: Elect according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit, unto obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: Grace unto you, and peace, be multiplied.
verses.note: Election by Father, sanctification by Spirit, sprinkling of Son's blood

verses.text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
verses.note: The Word was God — pre-existence and divine nature

verses.text: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.
verses.note: In Christ the fullness of the Godhead dwells bodily

verses.text: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
verses.note: The exact representation of God's being

verses.text: I and my Father are one.
verses.note: Jesus' claim to unity with the Father

subtopics.label: The Deity of the Holy Spirit

verses.text: But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back part of the price of the land?
verses.note: Lying to the Spirit equated with lying to God

verses.text: But God hath revealed them unto us by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God.
verses.note: The Spirit searches the deep things of God

verses.text: Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.
verses.note: The Lord is the Spirit

verses.text: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD:
verses.note: The Shema — the LORD our God is one LORD

verses.text: Come ye near unto me, hear ye this; I have not spoken in secret from the beginning; from the time that it was, there am I: and now the Lord GOD, and his Spirit, hath sent me.
verses.note: The Lord GOD and his Spirit have sent me

verses.text: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
verses.note: Father sends the Spirit in the Son's name

title: Divine Providence
description: God's continuous sovereign governance over all creation, sustaining, directing, and governing all events toward his appointed ends.

verses.text: And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.
verses.note: In him all things hold together

verses.text: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
verses.note: Upholding all things by the word of his power

verses.text: For in him we live, and move, and have our being; as certain also of your own poets have said, For we are also his offspring.
verses.note: In him we live and move and have our being

verses.text: A man's heart deviseth his way: but the LORD directeth his steps.
verses.note: Man plans his way but the LORD directs his steps

verses.text: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the LORD.
verses.note: The lot is cast but the decision is from the LORD

verses.text: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
verses.note: All things work together for good

verses.text: In whom also we have obtained an inheritance, being predestinated according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will:
verses.note: Works all things according to the counsel of his will

verses.text: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
verses.note: Not a sparrow falls without the Father

verses.text: He causeth the grass to grow for the cattle, and herb for the service of man: that he may bring forth food out of the earth;
verses.note: God causes grass to grow for the cattle

verses.text: That ye may be the children of your Father which is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
verses.note: He sends rain on the just and the unjust

title: The Sovereignty of God
description: God's absolute rule over all creation — his right and power to do all that he pleases, with nothing outside his authority or beyond his control.

verses.text: The LORD hath prepared his throne in the heavens; and his kingdom ruleth over all.
verses.note: The LORD has established his throne in the heavens

verses.text: And all the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing: and he doeth according to his will in the army of heaven, and among the inhabitants of the earth: and none can stay his hand, or say unto him, What doest thou?
verses.note: He does according to his will in the host of heaven

verses.text: Thine, O LORD, is the greatness, and the power, and the glory, and the victory, and the majesty: for all that is in the heaven and in the earth is thine; thine is the kingdom, O LORD, and thou art exalted as head above all.
verses.note: Thine is the greatness and the power and the glory

verses.text: Declaring the end from the beginning, and from ancient times the things that are not yet done, saying, My counsel shall stand, and I will do all my pleasure:
verses.note: Declaring the end from the beginning

verses.text: For the kingdom is the LORD'S: and he is the governor among the nations.
verses.note: The kingdom is the LORD's — he rules among the nations

verses.text: The king's heart is in the hand of the LORD, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.
verses.note: The king's heart is in the hand of the LORD

verses.text: And hath made of one blood all nations of men for to dwell on all the face of the earth, and hath determined the times before appointed, and the bounds of their habitation;
verses.note: He determined the times and boundaries of nations

verses.text: Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
verses.note: Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling

verses.text: For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.
verses.note: For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do

verses.text: Him, being delivered by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge of God, ye have taken, and by wicked hands have crucified and slain:
verses.note: Delivered by God's determined counsel, yet by wicked hands

title: The Incarnation
description: The eternal Son of God took on human nature, becoming fully man while remaining fully God, uniting two natures in one person.

verses.text: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.
verses.note: The Word was made flesh and dwelt among us

verses.text: Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God:
verses.note: Who being in the form of God thought it not robbery to be equal with God

verses.text: But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant, and was made in the likeness of men:
verses.note: Made himself of no reputation, took the form of a servant

verses.text: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.
verses.note: God was manifest in the flesh

verses.text: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,
verses.note: God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law

verses.text: And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.
verses.note: She brought forth her firstborn son

verses.text: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.
verses.note: A virgin shall conceive and bear a son

verses.text: Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.
verses.note: In all things made like unto his brethren

verses.text: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin.
verses.note: Tempted in all points like as we are, yet without sin

verses.text: Jesus wept.
verses.note: Jesus wept — genuine human emotion

title: Grace
description: God's unmerited favour toward sinners — the free and undeserved gift by which God redeems, sustains, and transforms those who trust in him.

verses.text: For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God:
verses.note: By grace are ye saved through faith, not of yourselves

verses.text: Not of works, lest any man should boast.
verses.note: Not of works, lest any man should boast

verses.text: Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:
verses.note: Justified freely by his grace through the redemption in Christ

verses.text: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
verses.note: Not by works of righteousness but by his mercy he saved us

verses.text: And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
verses.note: My grace is sufficient for thee

verses.text: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,
verses.note: The grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared

verses.text: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
verses.note: Teaching us to deny ungodliness and worldly lusts

verses.text: For the law was given by Moses, but grace and truth came by Jesus Christ.
verses.note: The law was given by Moses; grace and truth came by Jesus Christ

verses.text: For sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under the law, but under grace.
verses.note: Ye are not under the law but under grace

verses.text: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.
verses.note: If righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain

title: Justification
description: The judicial act of God by which sinners are declared righteous on the basis of Christ's atoning work, received through faith alone.

verses.text: Therefore being justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ:
verses.note: Being justified by faith, we have peace with God

verses.text: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.
verses.note: A man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law

verses.text: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.
verses.note: Not justified by the works of the law but by the faith of Jesus Christ

verses.text: But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.
verses.note: His faith is counted for righteousness

verses.text: For he hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.
verses.note: Made him to be sin for us, that we might be made the righteousness of God

verses.text: But for us also, to whom it shall be imputed, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead;
verses.note: Imputed to us also, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus

verses.text: And he believed in the LORD; and he counted it to him for righteousness.
verses.note: He believed in the LORD and he counted it to him for righteousness

verses.text: For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.
verses.note: Abraham believed God and it was counted unto him for righteousness

verses.text: And the scripture was fulfilled which saith, Abraham believed God, and it was imputed unto him for righteousness: and he was called the Friend of God.
verses.note: Abraham believed God and it was imputed unto him for righteousness

title: Sanctification
description: The progressive work of God and the believer by which the Christian is made increasingly free from sin and conformed to the image of Christ.

verses.text: Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ:
verses.note: He which hath begun a good work in you will perform it

verses.text: And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and I pray God your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.note: The God of peace sanctify you wholly

verses.text: Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord:
verses.note: Follow holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord

verses.text: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
verses.note: Present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable

verses.text: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
verses.note: Be not conformed to this world but be ye transformed

verses.text: Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God.
verses.note: Cleansing ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit

verses.text: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.
verses.note: Walk in the Spirit and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh

verses.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
verses.note: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace

verses.text: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
verses.note: If ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live

title: Predestination & Election
description: God's eternal decree by which he foreordained whatsoever comes to pass, including the election of certain individuals to salvation.

verses.text: According as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him in love:
verses.note: He hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world

verses.text: Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,
verses.note: Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children

verses.text: But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth:
verses.note: God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation

verses.text: And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him, whose names are not written in the book of life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
verses.note: Written in the book of life from the foundation of the world

verses.text: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
verses.note: Whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate

verses.text: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
verses.note: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called

verses.text: (For the children being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth;)
verses.note: The purpose of God according to election might stand

verses.text: For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.
verses.note: I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy

verses.text: Nay but, O man, who art thou that repliest against God? Shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou made me thus?
verses.note: Shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou made me thus?

verses.text: No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.note: No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him

title: Original Sin
description: The doctrine that all humanity inherits a sinful nature from Adam's fall, resulting in universal guilt, moral corruption, and spiritual death.

verses.text: And when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.
verses.note: She took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband

verses.text: Wherefore, as by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin; and so death passed upon all men, for that all have sinned:
verses.note: By one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin

verses.text: For as by one man's disobedience many were made sinners, so by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous.
verses.note: By one man's disobedience many were made sinners

verses.text: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;
verses.note: For all have sinned and come short of the glory of God

verses.text: As it is written, There is none righteous, no, not one:
verses.note: There is none righteous, no not one

verses.text: Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.
verses.note: Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me

verses.text: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked: who can know it?
verses.note: The heart is deceitful above all things and desperately wicked

verses.text: For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
verses.note: The wages of sin is death

verses.text: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.
verses.note: In the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die

verses.text: And you hath he quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins;
verses.note: You hath he quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins

title: The Resurrection
description: The bodily resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead on the third day, and the future resurrection of all believers to eternal life.

verses.text: For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures;
verses.note: Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures

verses.text: And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures:
verses.note: He was buried, and rose again the third day

verses.text: And declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead:
verses.note: Declared to be the Son of God with power by the resurrection

verses.text: Whom God hath raised up, having loosed the pains of death: because it was not possible that he should be holden of it.
verses.note: Whom God hath raised up, having loosed the pains of death

verses.text: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
verses.note: It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption

verses.text: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
verses.note: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body

verses.text: Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body, according to the working whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself.
verses.note: Who shall change our vile body, fashioned like his glorious body

verses.text: And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain, and your faith is also vain.
verses.note: If Christ be not risen then is our preaching vain

verses.text: And if Christ be not raised, your faith is vain; ye are yet in your sins.
verses.note: If Christ be not raised, your faith is vain; ye are yet in your sins

verses.text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
verses.note: Begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection

title: Salvation
description: God's comprehensive work of rescuing sinners from the penalty, power, and ultimately the presence of sin through the person and work of Jesus Christ.

verses.text: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;
verses.note: All have sinned and come short of the glory of God

verses.text: For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
verses.note: The wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life

verses.text: But your iniquities have separated between you and your God, and your sins have hid his face from you, that he will not hear.
verses.note: Your iniquities have separated between you and your God

verses.text: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
verses.note: I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me

verses.text: Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.
verses.note: Neither is there salvation in any other

verses.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
verses.note: God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son

verses.text: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
verses.note: Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God

verses.text: Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
verses.note: If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature

verses.text: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
verses.note: By the washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Ghost

title: Revelation & Inspiration
description: God's self-disclosure to humanity through creation, conscience, Scripture, and supremely in Jesus Christ, and the divine inspiration of the biblical text.

verses.text: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
verses.note: The heavens declare the glory of God

verses.text: For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even his eternal power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse:
verses.note: The invisible things of him are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made

verses.text: Nevertheless he left not himself without witness, in that he did good, and gave us rain from heaven, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.
verses.note: He left not himself without witness

verses.text: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:
verses.note: All scripture is given by inspiration of God

verses.text: For the prophecy came not in old time by the will of man: but holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost.
verses.note: Holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost

verses.text: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,
verses.note: God who at sundry times spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets

verses.text: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;
verses.note: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son

verses.text: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him.
verses.note: The only begotten Son hath declared him

verses.text: Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:
verses.note: Who is the image of the invisible God

title: The Omniscience of God
description: God's perfect and complete knowledge of all things — past, present, and future — including all actualities and all possibilities.

verses.text: For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.
verses.note: God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things

verses.text: Great is our Lord, and of great power: his understanding is infinite.
verses.note: Great is our Lord, and of great power: his understanding is infinite

verses.text: Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.
verses.note: All things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do

verses.text: Declaring the end from the beginning, and from ancient times the things that are not yet done, saying, My counsel shall stand, and I will do all my pleasure:
verses.note: Declaring the end from the beginning

verses.text: Known unto God are all his works from the beginning of the world.
verses.note: Known unto God are all his works from the beginning of the world

verses.text: For there is not a word in my tongue, but, lo, O LORD, thou knowest it altogether.
verses.note: There is not a word in my tongue, but lo, O LORD, thou knowest it altogether

verses.text: I the LORD search the heart, I try the reins, even to give every man according to his ways, and according to the fruit of his doings.
verses.note: I the LORD search the heart

verses.text: But the LORD said unto Samuel, Look not on his countenance, or on the height of his stature; because I have refused him: for the LORD seeth not as man seeth; for man looketh on the outward appearance, but the LORD looketh on the heart.
verses.note: The LORD looketh on the heart

verses.text: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me.
verses.note: O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me

verses.text: Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising, thou understandest my thought afar off.
verses.note: Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising

title: The Omnipotence of God
description: God's unlimited power to accomplish all that he wills — nothing is too hard for the Lord, and no purpose of his can be thwarted.

verses.text: Ah Lord GOD! behold, thou hast made the heaven and the earth by thy great power and stretched out arm, and there is nothing too hard for thee:
verses.note: There is nothing too hard for thee

verses.text: For with God nothing shall be impossible.
verses.note: For with God nothing shall be impossible

verses.text: But Jesus beheld them, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.
verses.note: With God all things are possible

verses.text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
verses.note: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth

verses.text: By the word of the LORD were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.
verses.note: By the word of the LORD were the heavens made

verses.text: For he spake, and it was done; he commanded, and it stood fast.
verses.note: He spake, and it was done; he commanded, and it stood fast

verses.text: (As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were.
verses.note: God who quickeneth the dead and calleth those things which be not as though they were

verses.text: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
verses.note: I am the resurrection and the life

verses.text: I am he that liveth, and was dead; and, behold, I am alive for evermore, Amen; and have the keys of hell and of death.
verses.note: I am he that liveth, and was dead; behold, I am alive for evermore

title: The Immutability of God
description: God does not change in his being, perfections, purposes, or promises — he is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

verses.text: For I am the LORD, I change not; therefore ye sons of Jacob are not consumed.
verses.note: For I am the LORD, I change not

verses.text: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and cometh down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning.
verses.note: The Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning

verses.text: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
verses.note: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever

verses.text: God is not a man, that he should lie; neither the son of man, that he should repent: hath he said, and shall he not do it? or hath he spoken, and shall he not make it good?
verses.note: God is not a man, that he should lie; neither the son of man, that he should repent

verses.text: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
verses.note: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever

verses.text: If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.
verses.note: If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself

title: Christology — The Person of Christ
description: The study of the person of Jesus Christ — his divine and human natures, his offices as prophet, priest, and king, and his mediatorial work.

verses.text: The LORD thy God will raise up unto thee a Prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like unto me; unto him ye shall hearken;
verses.note: The LORD thy God will raise up unto thee a Prophet

verses.text: Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our profession.
verses.note: We have a great high priest that is passed into the heavens

verses.text: And he hath on his vesture and on his thigh a name written, KING OF KINGS, AND LORD OF LORDS.
verses.note: He hath on his vesture a name written, KING OF KINGS

verses.text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
verses.note: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God

verses.text: Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.
verses.note: Before Abraham was, I am

verses.text: And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.
verses.note: He is before all things, and by him all things consist

verses.text: But thou, Bethlehem Ephratah, though thou be little among the thousands of Judah, yet out of thee shall he come forth unto me that is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings forth have been from of old, from everlasting.
verses.note: Whose goings forth have been from of old, from everlasting

verses.text: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;
verses.note: There is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus

verses.text: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first testament, they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance.
verses.note: He is the mediator of the new testament

verses.text: But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry, by how much also he is the mediator of a better covenant, which was established upon better promises.
verses.note: He is the mediator of a better covenant

title: The Holy Spirit
description: The person and work of the third person of the Trinity — his deity, personality, and ministry of conviction, regeneration, indwelling, and empowerment.

verses.text: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
verses.note: The Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send

verses.text: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.
verses.note: When he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth

verses.text: As they ministered to the Lord, and fasted, the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.
verses.note: The Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul

verses.text: And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
verses.note: He will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment

verses.text: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
verses.note: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit

verses.text: But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
verses.note: All these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit

verses.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
verses.note: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace

verses.text: What? know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own?
verses.note: Your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost

verses.text: In whom ye also trusted, after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,
verses.note: Ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise

verses.text: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
verses.note: If any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his

title: The Church
description: The nature, purpose, and marks of the church — the community of all believers united to Christ as his body, called to worship, witness, and service.

verses.text: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
verses.note: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular

verses.text: And hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be the head over all things to the church,
verses.note: Gave him to be the head over all things to the church

verses.text: And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.
verses.note: He is the head of the body, the church

verses.text: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
verses.note: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them

verses.text: And they continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers.
verses.note: They continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship

verses.text: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:
verses.note: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry

verses.text: And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone;
verses.note: Built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets

verses.text: In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord:
verses.note: In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple

verses.text: Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house, an holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God by Jesus Christ.
verses.note: Ye also as lively stones are built up a spiritual house

title: Imputation
description: The theological principle by which Adam's guilt is reckoned to humanity, humanity's sin is reckoned to Christ, and Christ's righteousness is reckoned to believers.

verses.text: Wherefore, as by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin; and so death passed upon all men, for that all have sinned:
verses.note: By one man sin entered into the world

verses.text: Therefore as by the offence of one judgment came upon all men to condemnation; even so by the righteousness of one the free gift came upon all men unto justification of life.
verses.note: By the offence of one, judgment came upon all men to condemnation

verses.text: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.
verses.note: In Adam all die

verses.text: All we like sheep have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way; and the LORD hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.
verses.note: The LORD hath laid on him the iniquity of us all

verses.text: For he hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.
verses.note: He hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin

verses.text: Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose stripes ye were healed.
verses.note: Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree

verses.text: But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.
verses.note: His faith is counted for righteousness

verses.text: For as by one man's disobedience many were made sinners, so by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous.
verses.note: By the obedience of one shall many be made righteous

verses.text: And be found in him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ, the righteousness which is of God by faith:
verses.note: The righteousness which is of God by faith

title: Propitiation
description: The satisfaction of God's righteous wrath against sin through Christ's atoning sacrifice — turning away divine judgment by absorbing it fully.

verses.text: And he is the propitiation for our sins: and not for ours only, but also for the sins of the whole world.
verses.note: He is the propitiation for our sins; and not for ours only, but for the whole world

verses.text: Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins.
verses.note: He loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins

verses.text: Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;
verses.note: Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood

subtopics.label: The Wrath of God Against Sin

verses.text: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who hold the truth in unrighteousness;
verses.note: The wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness

verses.text: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
verses.note: He that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him

verses.text: God is jealous, and the LORD revengeth; the LORD revengeth, and is furious; the LORD will take vengeance on his adversaries, and he reserveth wrath for his enemies.
verses.note: God is jealous, and the LORD revengeth

title: Regeneration
description: The supernatural work of the Holy Spirit by which a spiritually dead sinner is made alive — born again with a new nature and the capacity for faith and repentance.

verses.text: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
verses.note: Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God

verses.text: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.note: Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit

verses.text: Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever.
verses.note: Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible

verses.text: But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us,
verses.note: God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us

verses.text: Even when we were dead in sins, hath quickened us together with Christ, (by grace ye are saved;)
verses.note: Even when we were dead in sins, hath quickened us together with Christ

verses.text: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
verses.note: By the washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Ghost

verses.text: Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
verses.note: If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature

verses.text: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you: and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you an heart of flesh.
verses.note: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you

verses.text: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.
verses.note: In Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature

title: Glorification
description: The final stage of salvation in which believers are fully conformed to the image of Christ — freed from sin entirely and given resurrection bodies.

verses.text: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
verses.note: Whom he justified, them he also glorified

verses.text: For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
verses.note: The sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory

verses.text: When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.
verses.note: When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory

verses.text: Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.
verses.note: We shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is

verses.text: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
verses.note: Conformed to the image of his Son

verses.text: Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body, according to the working whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself.
verses.note: Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body

title: Perseverance of the Saints
description: The doctrine that those whom God has truly saved will persevere in faith to the end — kept by divine power, they cannot finally fall away.

verses.text: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.
verses.note: I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish

verses.text: My Father, which gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father's hand.
verses.note: No man is able to pluck them out of my Father's hand

verses.text: Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ:
verses.note: He which hath begun a good work in you will perform it

verses.text: Who are kept by the power of God through faith unto salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
verses.note: Kept by the power of God through faith

verses.text: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
verses.note: Neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers

verses.text: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
verses.note: Nor any other creature shall be able to separate us from the love of God

verses.text: Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy,
verses.note: Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling

verses.text: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,
verses.note: For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened

verses.text: For if after they have escaped the pollutions of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, they are again entangled therein, and overcome, the latter end is worse with them than the beginning.
verses.note: If after they have escaped the pollutions of the world through the knowledge of the Lord

verses.text: They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would no doubt have continued with us: but they went out, that they might be made manifest that they were not all of us.
verses.note: They went out from us, but they were not of us

title: Assurance of Salvation
description: The subjective confidence believers may have that they are truly saved — grounded in the promises of God, the witness of the Spirit, and the evidence of a changed life.

verses.text: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
verses.note: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God

verses.text: These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God.
verses.note: That ye may know that ye have eternal life

verses.text: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
verses.note: God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father

verses.text: We know that we have passed from death unto life, because we love the brethren. He that loveth not his brother abideth in death.
verses.note: We know that we have passed from death unto life, because we love the brethren

verses.text: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Know ye not your own selves, how that Jesus Christ is in you, except ye be reprobates?
verses.note: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith

verses.text: And hereby we do know that we know him, if we keep his commandments.
verses.note: Hereby we do know that we know him, if we keep his commandments

title: The Inspiration of Scripture
description: The divine superintendence of the biblical authors by which the Holy Spirit guided them to write exactly what God intended, resulting in an authoritative and inerrant text.

verses.text: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:
verses.note: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable

verses.text: Knowing this first, that no prophecy of the scripture is of any private interpretation.
verses.note: No prophecy of the scripture is of any private interpretation

verses.text: For the prophecy came not in old time by the will of man: but holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost.
verses.note: Holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost

verses.text: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;
verses.note: The scripture cannot be broken

verses.text: For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law, till all be fulfilled.
verses.note: Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law

verses.text: LAMED. For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven.
verses.note: For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven

verses.text: That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.
verses.note: That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works

verses.text: The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul: the testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple.
verses.note: The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul

verses.text: Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you, neither shall ye diminish ought from it, that ye may keep the commandments of the LORD your God which I command you.
verses.note: Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you

title: The Canon of Scripture
description: The fixed collection of 66 books recognized by the church as the authoritative Word of God — how the books were identified, received, and confirmed.

verses.text: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:
verses.note: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me

verses.text: For this cause also thank we God without ceasing, because, when ye received the word of God which ye heard of us, ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which effectually worketh also in you that believe.
verses.note: Ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God

verses.text: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.
verses.note: The Spirit of truth will guide you into all truth

verses.text: Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you, neither shall ye diminish ought from it, that ye may keep the commandments of the LORD your God which I command you.
verses.note: Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you

verses.text: For I testify unto every man that heareth the words of the prophecy of this book, If any man shall add unto these things, God shall add unto him the plagues that are written in this book:
verses.note: If any man shall add unto these things, God shall add unto him the plagues

verses.text: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.
verses.note: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee

title: The Aseity of God
description: God's self-existence and self-sufficiency — he depends on nothing outside himself for his being, and all things depend entirely on him.

verses.text: And God said unto Moses, I AM THAT I AM: and he said, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, I AM hath sent me unto you.
verses.note: I AM THAT I AM

verses.text: For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;
verses.note: As the Father hath life in himself, so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself

verses.text: Neither is worshipped with men's hands, as though he needed any thing, seeing he giveth to all life, and breath, and all things;
verses.note: Neither is worshipped with men's hands, as though he needed any thing

verses.text: For in him we live, and move, and have our being; as certain also of your own poets have said, For we are also his offspring.
verses.note: In him we live, and move, and have our being

verses.text: Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for thou hast created all things, and for thy pleasure they are and were created.
verses.note: Thou hast created all things, and for thy pleasure they are and were created

verses.text: For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him:
verses.note: All things were created by him, and for him

title: The Second Coming of Christ
description: The visible, bodily return of Jesus Christ in power and glory to judge the living and the dead, establish his kingdom, and consummate redemption.

verses.text: Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven? this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.
verses.note: This same Jesus shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go

verses.text: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.
verses.note: I will come again, and receive you unto myself

verses.text: So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.
verses.note: Unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time

verses.text: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
verses.note: They shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven

verses.text: Behold, he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him, and they also which pierced him: and all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him. Even so, Amen.
verses.note: Behold, he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him

verses.text: For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:
verses.note: The Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout

verses.text: Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.
verses.note: Be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh

verses.text: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
verses.note: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is

verses.text: For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
verses.note: The day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night

title: The Final Judgment
description: The universal judgment at the end of the age when all people will give account before God, and eternal destinies are sealed.

verses.text: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.
verses.note: Every one of us shall give account of himself to God

verses.text: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.
verses.note: We must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ

verses.text: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:
verses.note: It is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment

verses.text: For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
verses.note: The Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son

verses.text: Because he hath appointed a day, in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by that man whom he hath ordained; whereof he hath given assurance unto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead.
verses.note: He hath appointed a day, in the which he will judge the world in righteousness

verses.text: When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:
verses.note: When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him

verses.text: And before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats:
verses.note: Before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them

verses.text: And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is the book of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.
verses.note: I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened

verses.text: A fiery stream issued and came forth from before him: thousand thousands ministered unto him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him: the judgment was set, and the books were opened.
verses.note: The judgment was set, and the books were opened

title: Heaven
description: The eternal dwelling place of God and the redeemed — a state of unbroken fellowship with God, perfect joy, and the absence of all evil and suffering.

verses.text: In my Father's house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
verses.note: In my Father's house are many mansions; I go to prepare a place for you

verses.text: But now they desire a better country, that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.
verses.note: God hath prepared for them a city

verses.text: For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
verses.note: We have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens

verses.text: And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
verses.note: God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death

verses.text: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord GOD will wipe away tears from off all faces; and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the LORD hath spoken it.
verses.note: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord GOD will wipe away tears

verses.text: They shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more; neither shall the sun light on them, nor any heat.
verses.note: They shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more

verses.text: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
verses.note: Now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face

verses.text: And they shall see his face; and his name shall be in their foreheads.
verses.note: They shall see his face; and his name shall be in their foreheads

verses.text: Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.
verses.note: We shall see him as he is

title: Hell
description: The final state of the unredeemed — eternal separation from the gracious presence of God, under his just judgment against sin.

verses.text: And these shall go away into everlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternal.
verses.note: These shall go away into everlasting punishment

verses.text: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
verses.note: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched

verses.text: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
verses.note: Fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell

verses.text: And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.
verses.note: Whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire

verses.text: And death and hell were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
verses.note: Death and hell were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death

verses.text: But the fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable, and murderers, and whoremongers, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death.
verses.note: Shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone

verses.text: Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power;
verses.note: Punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord

verses.text: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.
verses.note: Depart from me, ye that work iniquity

verses.text: Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:
verses.note: Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire

title: The Resurrection of the Dead
description: The future bodily resurrection of all people — the righteous to eternal life and the unrighteous to judgment.

verses.text: Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,
verses.note: The hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice

verses.text: And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
verses.note: They that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation

verses.text: And many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt.
verses.note: Many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake

verses.text: And have hope toward God, which they themselves also allow, that there shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.
verses.note: There shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust

verses.text: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
verses.note: It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption

verses.text: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
verses.note: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory

verses.text: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.
verses.note: This corruptible must put on incorruption

title: The Millennium
description: The thousand-year reign of Christ described in Revelation 20 — variously interpreted as a future literal kingdom on earth, a present spiritual reign, or a symbolic representation of the church age.

verses.text: And I saw thrones, and they sat upon them, and judgment was given unto them: and I saw the souls of them that were beheaded for the witness of Jesus, and for the word of God, and which had not worshipped the beast, neither his image, neither had received his mark upon their foreheads, or in their hands; and they lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years.
verses.note: They lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years

verses.text: Blessed and holy is he that hath part in the first resurrection: on such the second death hath no power, but they shall be priests of God and of Christ, and shall reign with him a thousand years.
verses.note: Blessed and holy is he that hath part in the first resurrection

verses.text: And he laid hold on the dragon, that old serpent, which is the Devil, and Satan, and bound him a thousand years,
verses.note: He laid hold on the dragon, that old serpent, and bound him a thousand years

verses.text: The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid; and the calf and the young lion and the fatling together; and a little child shall lead them.
verses.note: The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb

verses.text: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together, and the lion shall eat straw like the bullock: and dust shall be the serpent's meat. They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain, saith the LORD.
verses.note: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together

verses.text: And the LORD shall be king over all the earth: in that day shall there be one LORD, and his name one.
verses.note: The LORD shall be king over all the earth

title: The Rapture
description: The sudden catching up of living believers to meet Christ in the air — debated as to its timing relative to the tribulation period.

verses.text: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
verses.note: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds

verses.text: For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:
verses.note: The Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout

verses.text: Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
verses.note: We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed

verses.text: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
verses.note: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump

verses.text: But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only.
verses.note: But of that day and hour knoweth no man

verses.text: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
verses.note: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left

verses.text: Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.
verses.note: Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come

title: New Heaven and New Earth
description: The final renewal of all creation — the present heavens and earth give way to a new, perfected cosmos where God dwells with his people forever.

verses.text: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away; and there was no more sea.
verses.note: I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away

verses.text: And he that sat upon the throne said, Behold, I make all things new. And he said unto me, Write: for these words are true and faithful.
verses.note: Behold, I make all things new

verses.text: Nevertheless we, according to his promise, look for new heavens and a new earth, wherein dwelleth righteousness.
verses.note: We, according to his promise, look for new heavens and a new earth

verses.text: And I heard a great voice out of heaven saying, Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and he will dwell with them, and they shall be his people, and God himself shall be with them, and be their God.
verses.note: Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and he will dwell with them

verses.text: And there shall be no more curse: but the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it; and his servants shall serve him:
verses.note: The throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it

verses.text: For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth: and the former shall not be remembered, nor come into mind.
verses.note: For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth

title: The Antichrist
description: The ultimate human adversary of Christ who will arise in the last days, embodying satanic opposition to God and deceiving many before being destroyed at Christ's return.

verses.text: Let no man deceive you by any means: for that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first, and that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition;
verses.note: That man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition

verses.text: Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.
verses.note: Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God

verses.text: Even him, whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,
verses.note: Whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs

verses.text: Little children, it is the last time: and as ye have heard that antichrist shall come, even now are there many antichrists; whereby we know that it is the last time.
verses.note: As ye have heard that antichrist shall come, even now are there many antichrists

verses.text: And every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is that spirit of antichrist, whereof ye have heard that it should come; and even now already is it in the world.
verses.note: Every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God

verses.text: For many deceivers are entered into the world, who confess not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh. This is a deceiver and an antichrist.
verses.note: Many deceivers are entered into the world

verses.text: And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming:
verses.note: The Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth

verses.text: And the beast was taken, and with him the false prophet that wrought miracles before him, with which he deceived them that had received the mark of the beast, and them that worshipped his image. These both were cast alive into a lake of fire burning with brimstone.
verses.note: The beast was taken, and the false prophet

title: The Tribulation
description: A period of unprecedented suffering and divine judgment before or concurrent with Christ's return — variously interpreted as future, past, or ongoing.

verses.text: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be.
verses.note: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world

verses.text: And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect's sake those days shall be shortened.
verses.note: Except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved

verses.text: Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:
verses.note: Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened

verses.text: And he shall confirm the covenant with many for one week: and in the midst of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the oblation to cease, and for the overspreading of abominations he shall make it desolate, even until the consummation, and that determined shall be poured upon the desolate.
verses.note: And he shall confirm the covenant with many for one week

verses.text: And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people: and there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation even to that same time: and at that time thy people shall be delivered, every one that shall be found written in the book.
verses.note: There shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation

verses.text: And I said unto him, Sir, thou knowest. And he said to me, These are they which came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
verses.note: These are they which came out of great tribulation

title: Signs of the Times
description: Events and conditions that Jesus and the apostles identified as markers of the end of the age — debated as to whether they are future, recurring, or being fulfilled now.

verses.text: And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
verses.note: Ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars

verses.text: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.
verses.note: Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom

verses.text: And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
verses.note: Many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many

verses.text: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.
verses.note: This gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world

verses.text: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.
verses.note: In the last days perilous times shall come

verses.text: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
verses.note: Men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters

verses.text: Knowing this first, that there shall come in the last days scoffers, walking after their own lusts,
verses.note: There shall come in the last days scoffers

title: Eternal Life
description: The quality and duration of life that belongs to all who are united to Christ — a present possession that extends into unending fellowship with God.

verses.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
verses.note: He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life

verses.text: And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.
verses.note: This is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God

verses.text: And this is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
verses.note: God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son

verses.text: He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life.
verses.note: He that hath the Son hath life

verses.text: For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
verses.note: The gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord

verses.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
verses.note: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life

verses.text: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.
verses.note: I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish

title: The Marriage Supper of the Lamb
description: The eschatological celebration described in Revelation 19 — the consummation of Christ's union with his bride, the church.

verses.text: Let us be glad and rejoice, and give honour to him: for the marriage of the Lamb is come, and his wife hath made herself ready.
verses.note: The marriage of the Lamb is come, and his wife hath made herself ready

verses.text: And he saith unto me, Write, Blessed are they which are called unto the marriage supper of the Lamb. And he saith unto me, These are the true sayings of God.
verses.note: Blessed are they which are called unto the marriage supper of the Lamb

verses.text: And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come.
verses.note: The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son

verses.text: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
verses.note: Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it

verses.text: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
verses.note: That he might present it to himself a glorious church

verses.text: For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
verses.note: I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ

verses.text: And I John saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
verses.note: The holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God, prepared as a bride

title: The Great White Throne
description: The final judgment scene of Revelation 20:11-15 where the dead stand before God and are judged according to their works, with the lake of fire as the final destination of the condemned.

verses.text: And I saw a great white throne, and him that sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away; and there was found no place for them.
verses.note: I saw a great white throne, and him that sat on it

verses.text: And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is the book of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.
verses.note: I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened

verses.text: And the sea gave up the dead which were in it; and death and hell delivered up the dead which were in them: and they were judged every man according to their works.
verses.note: The sea gave up the dead which were in it; and death and hell delivered up the dead

verses.text: And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is the book of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.
verses.note: The dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works

verses.text: And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.
verses.note: Whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire

verses.text: Who will render to every man according to his deeds:
verses.note: Who will render to every man according to his deeds
